perturbation.original_text: a. "Company" shall have the meaning assigned in the preamble.
perturbation.changed_text: a. "Company" shall refer to i3 Integrative Creative Solutions, LLC only.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition states that 'Company' has the meaning assigned in the preamble, where it is defined as Bravatek Solutions, Inc. (BVTK). The modified definition restricts the meaning of 'Company' to i3 Integrative Creative Solutions, LLC. This creates a direct contradiction, because other sections, such as the preamble and signature blocks, refer to 'Company' as Bravatek. This in-text contradiction introduces confusion about which entity is actually bound by the obligations and entitled to the benefits assigned to the 'Company' throughout the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT 10.1 RESELLER AGREEMENT THIS RESELLER AGREEMENT (this "Agreement") is made and entered into effect the 7th day of April, 2017 ("Effective Date"), by and between i3 Integrative Creative Solutions, LLC ("i3 ICS"), a Virginia limited liability company, having its offices at 6564 Loisdale Court Suite 1010B, Springfield, VA 22150 ("Reseller") and the company set forth below ("Company") (each, individually, a "party" and collectively, "parties"): Company: Bravatek Solutions, Inc. (BVTK) Telephone: 1-866-490-8590 Address:2028 E. Ben White Blvd., Suite 240-2835 Fax: N/A Austin, Texas 78741 E-mail: tom.cellucci@bravatek.com Territory: US Federal Government Civilian and Military Agencies/Customers in the U.S. Agreement Term: 1 Year Company Products: cybersecurity email software/telecom services Other Terms (not applicable if blank): Pricing: Reseller will obtain pricing quote from Company for each opportunity. Contract is renewable for 1 year extension by amendment to this agreement. Either Party may terminate this agreement for non-cause with a sixty (60) written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original definition in the original text states that the 'Company' has the meaning assigned in the preamble, which is Bravatek Solutions, Inc. (BVTK). The changed text redefines 'Company' to mean only i3 Integrative Creative Solutions, LLC. This directly contradicts the preamble in the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly states that the 'Company' is Bravatek Solutions, Inc. This change creates a clear and impactful contradiction as to which entity is bound by the agreement.

perturbation.original_text:
Reseller will be compensated for Registered Referrals and Registered Opportunities (as defined in Section 1g, 1i.) based on the following schedule:
Registered Referral: · 25% of Net Revenue (as defined in Section 1e.) with a COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPON SALE PRICE in writing, the case of COMPANY's software products. The Company's Telecom Services will be agreed upon on a case-by-case basis in writing.
perturbation.changed_text:
Reseller will be compensated for Registered Referrals and Registered Opportunities (as defined in Section 1g, 1i.) based on the following schedule:
Registered Referral: · 25% of Net Revenue (as defined in Section 1e.) with a COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPON SALE PRICE in writing, the case of COMPANY's software products. The Company's Telecom Services will be compensated based on Company's discretion and at a rate determined by the Company at the time of sale.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that compensation for telecom services will be agreed upon in writing on a case-by-case basis. The change introduces ambiguity, by stating that compensation will be at a rate determined by the Company at the time of sale, at the Company's discretion. This contradicts the previous statement requiring written agreement, creating uncertainty about the compensation process and giving the Company unilateral control. This is contradictory to what is being said in section 6: 'In the event that Reseller (i) refers a Registered Referral to Company, through delivery to Company of the lead form attached hereto as Exhibit A, and (ii) such Registered Referral executes an agreement to purchase any of such Company Products or services, then Company shall pay Reseller a Referral Fee, as specified in Exhibit B attached hereto.' because that referral fee now depends on the discretion of the company and it's 'determined' rate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that Reseller (i) refers a Registered Referral to Company, through delivery to Company of the lead form attached hereto as Exhibit A, and (ii) such Registered Referral executes an agreement to purchase any of such Company Products or services, then Company shall pay Reseller a Referral Fee, as specified in Exhibit B attached hereto. Referral Fees will be paid within thirty (30) days of the collection of receivables from the Registered Referral. In the event an opportunity is requested and the government elects to use a different contract vehicle as described in 1g, reseller will still receive Referral Fee outlined in exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that compensation for telecom services will be agreed upon in writing on a case-by-case basis. The changed text states that compensation will be at a rate determined by the Company at the time of sale, at the Company's discretion. This directly contradicts the original agreement requiring written agreement, and introducing ambiguity about the compensation process. This contradicts the 'Referral Fee, as specified in Exhibit B' statement in the 'Contradicted Text' because the referral fee now depends on the discretion of the company.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Company shall notify reseller of any changes, modifications or the discontinuation of any of the Company Products within thirty (30) days. Company shall make reasonable accommodations for any ongoing sales or existing sales.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Company may notify reseller of any changes, modifications or the discontinuation of any of the Company Products. Company shall make reasonable accommodations for any ongoing sales or existing sales.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Company 'shall notify' the reseller of changes to the products within thirty (30) days. The changed text states the Company 'may notify' the reseller of any changes, and removes the timeframe. This contradiction causes uncertainty because, section 3 says: 'Reseller shall have the right to solicit orders for Company Products within the Territory. In its activities under this Agreement, Reseller shall use the then-current names for the Company Products and any sales or marketing documentation or forms provided by Company.' which indicates the reseller is oblied to use the products and documentations provided by the company. If there are any changes and the company does not notify them as the original stated, creates a In-text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Reseller shall have the right to solicit orders for Company Products within the Territory. In its activities under this Agreement, Reseller shall use the then-current names for the Company Products and any sales or marketing documentation or forms provided by Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the Company to notify the reseller of changes within a specific timeframe (30 days). This obligation is crucial for the reseller to comply with its duty, as stated in the contradicted text, to use "the then-current names for the Company Products and any sales or marketing documentation or forms provided by Company." By changing 'shall notify' to 'may notify' and removing the timeframe, the modification introduces ambiguity and allows the company to not notify the reseller of changes. This directly contradicts the reseller's obligation to use current product information. If the company does not notify the reseller of product changes, the reseller cannot fulfill its obligation, creating a direct, impactful contradiction that would cause confusion.
